Question: What is a fancy and redundant way of referring to a will, the written document providing for the disposition of a person's property after death?

What legal defense would be used on behalf of Mr. Martin in this case?   Mr. Martin is a bank president charged with   robbing his own bank.  But he and his family   were kidnapped and he was told that unless   he brought all of the cash in the bank vault   to the kidnappers, his family would be killed.
Name the written instrument that conveys an interest in real estate from one person to another.

Lawyers will present facts such as youthfulness, extreme anxiety, or mental deficiency in an attempt to influence the court to lighten the sentence of a convicted person.  What adjective describes these sorts of circumstances?

A rock group was on its way to a sold-out concert but did not show up.  The concert promoters were angry, but the rock group was released from any legal consequences for not appearing because their bus was blown off the road by a tornado, a circumstance called an act of what?
The name for what legal defense was derived from a Latin word meaning "elsewhere" or "somewhere else"?
An order issued by an administrative agency directing an individual or a corporation to refrain from some proscribed business practice is a ---- order.
What kind of official statement summarizes the possible adverse consequences of a proposed project on the people, wildlife, air, land, and water affected by it?
A document that transfers ownership of a piece of personal property from one person to another in exchange for money is called a bill of what?
A defendant who fails the "right-and-wrong" test or the "irresistible-impulse test" may not be held responsible for crimes by reason of what?

Sometimes in relation to charges of violence, assault, and even murder, the defendant admits responsibility for the crime but claims it was justified by the other person's threatening actions.  Name this legal defense.
The Nuremberg Defense is a legal defense that essentially asserts the defendant was only doing what?
What document is the official record of the time, date, and place you were born?
Backed only by the credit worthiness of the debtor, what is the name for a certificate issued by a person or business entity for the purpose of acknowledging indebtedness?

What defense consists of a statement by an individual charged with a crime that he or she was so distant when the crime was committed that participation in the commission of that crime was impossible?
What defense, often used in date rape cases, is a claim of assumed permission or sanction?
The president or a state governor may release a convicted criminal by issuing what document?
A mental state provoked by fear, rage, anger or terror that, combined with adequate provocation, is an element in certain crimes like voluntary manslaughter is called the heat of what?
Ralph Niedermeyer is accused of stealing a plutonium shipment.  The prosecuting attorney asks him in court just how he pulled it off.  What amendment should Ralph invoke at this time?

When Vince Ricardo was accused of starting a forest fire near Salmon, Idaho last June, he invoked what defense when he said that he was in South America at the time with Jose Grecos de Muertos studying giant tsetse flies?

What legal defense is illustrated in this example?   Assume it is true that the defendant is guilty.   Then he must have been at the scene of the   crime.  But today we proved that he was on a   farm 25 miles away.
Dick Crabtree was accused of poaching a deer in Michigan in August, but Dick could prove he was actually visiting his mom in Alabama at the time the offense was committed.   Name this legal defense.
What is the Latin phrase for "not of sound mind" and therefore not legally responsible?
A person who lacks the capacity to understand the consequences or nature of a particular act or who is unable to distinguish right from wrong may use what defense in a court of law?
Leslie Bonnano was charged with a hit in Chicago on May 2nd of 1991.  But Leslie truthfully said she was visiting her aged godfather in Sicily at the time.  Name this legal defense.
What is the synonym for the legal defense called coercion?
You are selling your boat and have written up a bill of sale.  You may need the seal of what officer to witness your signature affixed to this instrument?
For what does the abbreviation stand in this?   An LOI is typically a nonbinding agreement   documenting the general terms of a proposed   business relationship.
While being mugged, Arnold picked up a convenient baseball bat and broke his assailant's arm.  If the mugger were to press charges for assault, what phrase should Arnold use in response?
Any federal construction project must file a report about its effects on such things as water and air quality, wildlife habitat, endangered species, and archaeological sites.  Name this summary.
For what does the abbreviation stand in this?   An NDA is a legal contract that specifies   confidential information that is not to be   shared with third parties.
Name the defense in which an attorney attempts to prove that an eyewitness to a crime did not see the defendant because, in fact, the person seen was someone else.
A document that forms a contract between the shipper and carrier as well as a receipt for the goods at the destination is called a bill of what?
The federal government and the governments of each of the states have constitutions.  What corresponding document does a city have?
An assault or murder resulting from a sudden, overwhelming impulse such as rage or grief, rather than committed as a premeditated act, is sometimes called a crime of what?
What document in the U.S. is analogous to the Institutes of Justinian, the Decalogue, the Code of Hammurabi, and the Magna Carta?

This is an example of what kind of legal document issued to a sheriff?   You are commanded to serve the herein   warrant and take into custody Robert Blythe   whose home address is 771 Sandhill Street   in Central City and bring forth said person   before this court.

Mabel Snodgrass claimed that after the meteorite fell on her head, she not only didn't remember robbing the bank but didn't even remember her own name or where she lived.  What term is used in psychology for this defense or mental condition?
Name the legal defense that applies to a person who commits an illegal act while sleepwalking.
U.S. citizens receive what kind of official notice requiring them to appear at a certain place and time for jury duty?
Identify the legal defense in which a defendant seeks to show that he would not have committed an unlawful act had he not been tricked into doing it by law enforcement officials.
Name the corporate document that typically specifies the number of members on the board, how they are to be selected, and when the meetings are to take place.
Defense lawyers sometimes argue that their clients should not be held fully liable for a crime because their mental functions were impaired at the time.  This legal defense is called diminished what?
What document determines that Dan Dillinger definitely does own a dilapidated domicile on Doddering Drive in downtown Detroit?
If an attorney wanted to review a witness's previous testimony, she could refer to what written record of the proceedings?
Reminiscent of a type of heavy transportation, what slang term indicates that a person has been convicted without an opportunity to present an adequate defense?
What document filed with the state or county health department lists a mother and father and contains the place, date, and time of a child's entry into the world?
A bank robber jumps into your car and orders you at gunpoint to speed away from the cops, who soon capture you.  You will not be charged with any crime because you were acting under what?

Mr. Carlysle came upon a gang brutally assaulting an aged storekeeper, and while trying to protect him, he clubbed one of the gang members and killed him.  If Mr. Carlysle were arrested charged with homicide, what legal defense would probably be invoked?
What kind of legal document can require someone to testify or submit requested documents in court?
What kind of note do you sign when you borrow money and pledge to pay back specified amounts on specified dates?
Name the procedural defense that forbids a defendant from being tried a second time for a crime, after having already been tried for the same crime.
A document by which you appoint another as your agent and confer upon that person the authority to perform specified acts on your behalf is a power of what?

The admitted killer of San Francisco Mayor George Moscone got only a manslaughter conviction on the basis that his mental condition was influenced by the sugar in his body from consuming Twinkies.  This defense is based on diminished what?
Through what document does a person determine the disposition of his property upon the event of his death?
What kind of court order requires a party to do or to refrain from doing specific acts?
What kind of document issued by a judge allows law enforcement personnel to look inside private property?
What is the term for a written sworn statement made under oath before a notary public?
What document formally establishes a person's identity with a sealed photograph, indicates nationality, requests safe passage for the traveler, and contain one or more visas?
Recall elections are initiated by the signatures of a large number of voters on what kind of document?
Name the document that demonstrates one's legal ownership to an item such as a motorcycle or truck.
A news agency managed to acquire secret government documents and publish them.  This act may be defended using which amendment?

This is an example of what kind of writ?   We command you that the body of Myrtle   Myers, in your custody detained, together   with the day and cause of her caption and   detention, be brought before the Honorable   J. Fred Muggs, United States Circuit Judge   of the Fifth Judicial Circuit.
With a name reminiscent of reflecting surfaces, what kind of wills drawn by a husband and wife are identical except that each leaves the same gifts to the other and each names the other as executor?
Name the annually renewed document carried in an automobile listing information about the vehicle's ownership.
What is the name for a section of any legal instrument such as a corporate charter, the Constitution, a treaty, or a will?
Many documents must be witnessed and stamped by what officer who is also empowered by state law to administer oaths and to witness signatures?
What is the legal term for a solemn declaration that something is true?
What is the term for a document used to submit a legal contention or argument to a court of law?
What document is given as permission by a foreign government to visitors entering that country?

Occasionally a criminal defendant claims that, at the time of the crime, he was of diminished mental capacity due to the intake of too much sugar.  Named after a popular cream-filled snack, this is known as what defense?
